sourceMovie.title: The Lost Bus
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Lost Bus for the same reason it broke through in 2025: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a history rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Paul Greengrass keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Silence of the Lambs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lost Bus clicked for you, The Silence of the Lambs (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lost Bus clicked for you, Blade Runner (1982) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lost Bus?
faq.answer: Try The Silence of the Lambs, Blade Runner, Braveheart—each shares DNA with The Lost Bus while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lost Bus?
faq.answer: The Lost Bus (2025) is best described as Thriller, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lost Bus worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
